attributes.name: Process Injection - Privileged Handle to EDR Agent Process
attributes.description: Detects attempts to open a process handle with write or thread-creation privileges to EDR agent processes such as MsSense.exe or SenseIR.exe. Attackers use OpenProcess with PROCESS_VM_WRITE, PROCESS_VM_OPERATION, or PROCESS_CREATE_THREAD to gain the ability to inject code or patch memory in security agent processes.
attributes.query:
process where event.action == "OpenProcess" and
  process.Ext.api.parameters.desired_access_numeric & 0x002A != 0 and
  process.Ext.api.target.process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe", "SenseCncProxy.exe") and
  not process.executable : ("?:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\\*", "?:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Security Client\\*")

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: Legitimate security tools performing process inspection
System debugging tools used by administrators
Software compatibility testing frameworks
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
This rule detects the core technique behavior: acquiring privileged handles to EDR processes. The desired_access bitmask 0x002A covers PROCESS_VM_WRITE (0x0020), PROCESS_VM_OPERATION (0x0008), and PROCESS_CREATE_THREAD (0x0002).

attributes.name: Memory Manipulation - WriteProcessMemory Targeting Security Process
attributes.description: Detects WriteProcessMemory API calls targeting EDR or security agent processes. This is a critical indicator of process injection or in-memory patching attacks designed to disable security controls by modifying running code in security-critical processes.
attributes.query:
api where event.action == "WriteProcessMemory" and
  process.Ext.api.target.process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe", "csfalcon.exe", "CylanceSvc.exe", "cb.exe") and
  not process.executable : ("?:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\\*", "?:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe")
false_positives: Legitimate Windows Update patching of security modules
Antivirus software performing memory scanning with write-back
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
This rule detects any tool writing to security process memory, a technique used to patch certificate validation functions like CRYPT32!CertVerifyCertificateChainPolicy.

attributes.name: Memory Scanning - ReadProcessMemory and VirtualQueryEx Against EDR Process
attributes.description: Detects memory enumeration and reading operations targeting EDR agent processes. Attackers enumerate memory regions with VirtualQueryEx and read function prologues with ReadProcessMemory to locate functions to patch, such as certificate validation routines in CRYPT32.dll.
attributes.query:
sequence by process.entity_id with maxspan=2m
  [api where event.action == "VirtualQueryEx" and
    process.Ext.api.target.process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe")]
  [api where event.action == "ReadProcessMemory" and
    process.Ext.api.target.process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe")]
false_positives: Debugging tools attached to processes
Performance monitoring software that reads process memory
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: Medium
Sequence rule: first memory region enumeration, then memory read of EDR process within 2 minutes. This pattern precedes memory patching attacks.

attributes.name: EDR Process Discovery - Enumeration of Security Agent Processes
attributes.description: Detects process creation events that enumerate or query EDR agent processes (MsSense.exe, SenseIR.exe). Attackers enumerate security processes to identify targets for injection or tampering. This includes use of CreateToolhelp32Snapshot, tasklist, wmic, or PowerShell Get-Process targeting security agents.
attributes.query:
any where
  (
    (event.category : "process" and event.action : "start" and
      process.command_line : ("*MsSense*", "*SenseIR*", "*SenseCncProxy*") and
      process.name in ("tasklist.exe", "wmic.exe", "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe")) or
    (event.action == "CreateToolhelp32Snapshot" and
      process.name != "MsSense.exe" and process.name != "SenseIR.exe")
  )

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: IT administrators checking service status
Monitoring scripts that verify MDE health
SCCM or Intune compliance checks
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: Medium
This rule detects the reconnaissance phase where attackers identify MDE processes before attempting injection.

attributes.name: Network Proxy Manipulation - WinHTTP Proxy Configuration Change
attributes.description: Detects netsh winhttp proxy configuration changes, which attackers use to intercept EDR agent communications. By setting a local proxy, an attacker can perform adversary-in-the-middle attacks against MDE cloud communication, potentially capturing or modifying telemetry and commands.
attributes.query:
process where event.action == "start" and
  process.name == "netsh.exe" and
  process.command_line : "*winhttp*" and
  process.command_line : "*proxy*" and
  process.command_line : "*set*"

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: Legitimate proxy configuration by IT administrators
Automated proxy setup scripts during VPN connection
Corporate proxy deployment via Group Policy
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
Detects the technique of manipulating WinHTTP proxy to intercept EDR communications. Any netsh winhttp proxy change outside of scheduled maintenance windows should be investigated.

attributes.name: Unauthenticated MDE API Access - Non-MDE Process Contacting Cloud Endpoints
attributes.description: Detects network connections to Microsoft Defender for Endpoint cloud gateway endpoints (winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com) from processes other than the legitimate MDE agent. Attackers exploit unauthenticated API endpoints to intercept commands, steal tokens, or exfiltrate configuration data.
attributes.query:
network where event.action == "connection_attempted" and
  destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and
  not process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe", "SenseCncProxy.exe", "SenseNdr.exe") and
  not process.executable : "?:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\\*"

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: Microsoft Defender updates through alternative update mechanisms
Rare legitimate Microsoft diagnostic tools
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
Only the MDE agent should contact winatp-gw endpoints. Any other process doing so indicates API abuse, command interception, or configuration exfiltration attempts.

attributes.name: MDE Command Interception - Rapid Requests to CnC Endpoint
attributes.description: Detects rapid sequential network connections to MDE command-and-control endpoints, indicating a race condition attack attempting to steal commands before the legitimate MDE agent processes them. The attacker sends burst requests to /edr/commands/cnc to intercept isolation commands, scan directives, or forensic collection tasks.
attributes.query:
sequence by process.entity_id with maxspan=10s
  [network where destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and destination.port == 443]
  [network where destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and destination.port == 443]
  [network where destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and destination.port == 443]
  [network where destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and destination.port == 443]
  [network where destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and destination.port == 443]

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: MDE agent performing rapid health checks during onboarding
Network scanning tools performing service discovery
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
Detects burst pattern of 5+ connections to MDE cloud within 10 seconds from a single process, characteristic of command interception race conditions.

attributes.name: CloudLR Token Abuse - Unauthorized Live Response Endpoint Access
attributes.description: Detects network connections to SenseIR Live Response endpoints from processes other than the legitimate SenseIR.exe agent. Attackers attempt to generate unauthorized CloudLR tokens that grant command execution, file download, and file upload capabilities on the endpoint.
attributes.query:
network where event.action == "connection_attempted" and
  destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and
  not process.name in ("SenseIR.exe", "MsSense.exe") and
  not process.executable : "?:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection\\*"

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: Microsoft diagnostic tools during MDE troubleshooting
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
CloudLR tokens provide powerful capabilities (command_execution, file_download, file_upload). Unauthorized token generation is a critical security event.

attributes.name: Multi-Phase EDR Attack - Process Injection Followed by Network API Abuse
attributes.description: Detects a correlated attack chain where a process first attempts to access an EDR agent process (handle acquisition or memory operation) and then makes network connections to MDE cloud endpoints. This sequence indicates a combined process injection and API exploitation attack against MDE.
attributes.query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=10m
  [process where event.action == "start" and
    process.command_line : ("*MsSense*", "*OpenProcess*", "*CRYPT32*", "*CertVerify*")]
  [network where destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and
    not process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe", "SenseCncProxy.exe")]

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: Extremely unlikely in legitimate operations - investigate immediately
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: High
This behavioral correlation rule detects the full MDE attack chain: process tampering followed by API exploitation. Any match should be treated as a critical incident.

attributes.name: Large Data Transfer from EDR Cloud Endpoint - Configuration Exfiltration
attributes.description: Detects unusually large data transfers from MDE cloud endpoints to non-MDE processes. Attackers exfiltrate the full MDE configuration file (~8MB) which contains detection rules, process exclusions, monitored behaviors, and certificate pins, providing a blueprint for further evasion.
attributes.query:
network where event.action in ("connection_attempted", "disconnect_received") and
  destination.domain : "winatp-gw-*.microsoft.com" and
  network.bytes > 5000000 and
  not process.name in ("MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe", "SenseCncProxy.exe")

technique.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

false_positives: MDE agent downloading large policy updates
Initial MDE onboarding configuration download
attributes.note:
Test ID: fec68e9b-af59-40c1-abbd-98ec98428444
Confidence: Medium
The MDE configuration file is approximately 8MB and contains detection rules, exclusions, and monitored processes. Unauthorized retrieval provides attackers with a detection evasion blueprint.
